target: Implement live session verification and degraded-state detection from tmux inspection and shared state mapping.
invariants: Preserve the tmux-host repo structure under src/tmux_host/ and keep subsystem boundaries intact.
Do not reintroduce Dopemux-specific ownership into host core; Dopemux remains an integration profile.
Keep desktop, mobile, CLI, and tmux-native surfaces consuming the same derived state model where UI/state files are touched.
Do not broaden allowlists or mix unrelated cleanup into these packets.
State remains bounded to ready, degraded, missing, and unknown with attached as an overlay.
Verification must be derived from live tmux inspection and metadata, not stale marker files.

commit.message: Implement live session verification and degraded-state detection

steps.task: Document the status model and implement verification primitives that classify missing, ready, degraded, and unknown states from live session inspection.
requirements: Keep status names exact and shared across CLI, tmux, desktop, and mobile.
Do not add synthetic aggregate health scores.
Use metadata and pane materialization facts as primary inputs.

steps.task: Implement degraded-state detection and recent event/state mapping so failures become visible to diagnostics and UI consumers.
requirements: Keep event severities bounded to info, warning, error, and success.
Do not implement recovery actions in this packet.
Doctor output must remain procedural and exact.

steps.task: Add integration tests that prove session verification and status mapping work for ready, degraded, missing, and unknown cases.
requirements: Use exact case fixtures for each status.
Ensure dashboard-facing mapping consumes the same status outputs.
